seo.metaDescription: Navigate Next.js 15 application documentation with role-based learning paths covering component system, hydration, server actions, and more.

blocks.content: The App Reference contains everything needed to understand, develop, and deploy the Next.js 15 application. Choose the path that matches your role and goals.
If you're new to the codebase or Next.js 15, follow this path to understand frontend fundamentals:

blocks.title: Server-First Pattern
blocks.content:
The application follows a server-first pattern where rendering, data fetching, and security checks happen on the server by default. Client-side interactivity is added only where needed.

This approach improves performance, security, and SEO while reducing JavaScript bundle size.

blocks.title: Defense-in-Depth Security
blocks.content:
Security is implemented at multiple layers: server-side validation, Zod schema validation, React Server Actions, CSRF protection, and runtime type guards.

No single point of failure - compromising one layer doesn't expose the system.

blocks.title: Related Documentation
blocks.content: For component implementation details, see the Component System guide. For backend integration patterns, see the CMS Reference section.
